title: Volume Snapshot Classes — The VolumeSnapshotClass Resource
text:
Each VolumeSnapshotClass contains the fields
driver
,
deletionPolicy
, and
parameters
,
which are used when a VolumeSnapshot belonging to the class needs to be
dynamically provisioned.
The name of a VolumeSnapshotClass object is significant, and is how users can
request a particular class. Administrators set the name and other parameters
of a class when first creating VolumeSnapshotClass objects, and the objects cannot
be updated once they are created.
apiVersion
:
snapshot.storage.k8s.io/v1
kind
:
VolumeSnapshotClass
metadata
:
name
:
csi-hostpath-snapclass
driver
:
hostpath.csi.k8s.io
deletionPolicy
:
Delete
parameters
:
Administrators can specify a default VolumeSnapshotClass for VolumeSnapshots
that don't request any particular class to bind to by adding the
snapshot.storage.kubernetes.io/is-default-class: "true"
annotation:
apiVersion
:
snapshot.storage.k8s.io/v1
kind
:
VolumeSnapshotClass
metadata
:
name
:
csi-hostpath-snapclass
annotations
:
snapshot.storage.kubernetes.io/is-default-class
:
"true"
driver
:
hostpath.csi.k8s.io
deletionPolicy
:
Delete
parameters
:
If multiple CSI drivers exist, a default VolumeSnapshotClass can be specified
for each of them.
